name: Assyrian Neo-Aramaic

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Assyrian Neo-Aramaic (assy1241).

notes: Skeleton. Assyrian Neo-Aramaic, a modern Semitic (Afroasiatic, Northeastern Neo-Aramaic) language written in the East Syriac abjad. Consonant letters are mapped to their modern IPA values; ⟨ܚ⟩ is realized as [x] and ⟨ܬ⟩ as [t]. Vowels are indicated only by optional pointing and the matres lectionis ⟨ܐ ܘ ܝ⟩, so unpointed text leaves most vowels unrecovered and a high PER floor is expected. Additional phonemes written with diacritic dots (e.g. [dʒ], [ʒ]) and gemination and stress are left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Assyrian Neo-Aramaic (assy1241)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
